mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate over the prior 3 months raises equity discount rates and tightens financial conditions, reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the signal is evaluated continuously.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month changes in FEDFUNDS do not predict lower 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if triggered negative-rate-momentum periods produce positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of walk-forward cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the policy rate crosses above a restrictive threshold, equity valuations compress as cash yields become competitive with expected equity returns, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day forward S&P 500 returns after FEDFUNDS crossings above 4.5% are not below unconditional 5-day returns, or if the hit rate for negative returns is below 50% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth combined with a stable or falling unemployment rate signals resilient labor income and near-term consumption support, increasing expected S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises over 3 months and UNRATE does not rise over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage value was 155350.0 and UNRATE was 3.6 on 2023-02-01
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with resilient labor demand
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS increases and non-rising UNRATE fail to generate above-baseline 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the interaction term has non-positive out-of-sample predictive contribution.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor demand and rising recession risk, reducing expected corporate earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if episodes with UNRATE rising more than 0.3pp over 3 months do not show below-baseline 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if negative-return frequency is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand and future construction activity, lowering cyclical earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 10% over 6 months
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day S&P 500 returns following 6-month HOUST declines greater than 10% are not lower than unconditional returns, or if the effect disappears in post-2005 walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in industrial production indicates improving real output and operating leverage for cyclical firms, supporting higher earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth turns positive after being negative
failure_condition: Reject if positive turns in 3-month INDPRO growth do not predict above-baseline 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if average forward returns are negative in triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth exceeding consumer price inflation signals rising real consumer demand, improving revenue expectations for listed firms and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage value was 697876.0 and CPIAUCSL was 301.648 on 2023-02-01
failure_condition: Reject if the RSAFS-minus-CPIAUCSL growth interaction has non-positive out-of-sample relation with 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if triggered periods do not outperform the unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation tightening regime, strong retail sales reinforce expectations of further monetary restraint, so demand strength raises discount-rate pressure more than earnings expectations and produces negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 12-month growth exceeds 4% and FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.5pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage value was 301.648 and FEDFUNDS was 4.57 on 2023-02-01
failure_condition: Reject if strong RSAFS observations during high CPIAUCSL inflation and rising FEDFUNDS do not produce below-baseline 5-day S&P 500 returns, or if the sign becomes positive in rolling walk-forward tests.
